responseMessageContents.text:
## Goal
- Fix IVX app startup reliability (black screen, infinite loading, crash) and fix automatic owner sign-in on cold launch so the login screen appears every time, then build and ship APKs proving both fixes

## Constraints & Preferences
- ONLY the existing Expo React Native app is approved ‚Äî no separate Kotlin app, no second Expo project, no replacement shell
- Approved Android package: `com.ivxholdings.app` (NOT `com.rork.ivxholdings` ‚Äî that Kotlin app is frozen)
- Use `bun x` not `npx` (npx not allowed in sandbox)
- Never ask user for credentials ‚Äî find them in project or work around blocker
- No secret values in logs, screenshots, reports ‚Äî redact all tokens/keys
- Owner email: `iperez4242@gmail.com`
- API base: `https://api.ivxholding.com`
- EXPO_TOKEN and EXPO_PUBLIC_EAS_PROJECT_ID are NOT set in sandbox ‚Äî EAS cloud build blocked, must build locally
- Diagnostic banner must be hidden by default in ALL runtimes, available only through Owner Control ‚Üí Diagnostics
- Owner AI send button must be disabled until `SIGNED_IN_OWNER` auth state
- Do NOT create new providers or architecture ‚Äî modify the EXISTING codebase only
- User demanded: exact file, line number, function name, root cause, code change, commit SHA, build ID, new APK, Android logcat, screenshot, confirmation app opens in under 5 seconds
- Startup requirements: max 5s startup timeout, visible error screen on init failure, try/catch on every startup task, log failing module, no network blocking during startup, auth must init async without freezing UI
- APK download link MUST be a direct `.apk` file (application/octet-stream), NOT an HTML landing page

## Progress
### Done
- Build 16 auth context fixes (still present):
  - `initAuth()` calls `supabase.auth.signOut({ scope: 'local' })` on cold launch, sets `manualOwnerLoginRef.current = false`
  - `onAuthStateChange` gated by `manualOwnerLoginRef` ‚Äî ignores all session events unless manual login occurred
  - `manualOwnerLoginRef` set unconditionally in `login()`, `loginOwnerPasswordless()`, `verify2FA()`
- Build 17 router auth guard added to `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`:
  - `useEffect` redirects to `/login` when `!isAuthenticated && !isLoading && !isOpenAccessModeEnabled()`
  - Loading spinner while `isLoading` is true
  - `redirectAttemptedRef` prevents duplicate redirects, resets when authenticated
- Build 18 startup reliability fixes applied to 3 files:
  - **`expo/app/_layout.tsx`**: Added `expo-splash-screen` import, `SplashScreen.preventAutoHideAsync()` at module scope, `SplashScreen.hideAsync()` in `useEffect`, 5-second `STARTUP_TIMEOUT_MS` safety timer showing visible error screen with Retry button, `injectWebKeyboardCSS()` wrapped in try/catch, startup error state with fallback UI, all deferred module loading wrapped in try/catch
  - **`expo/lib/auth-context.tsx`**: `initAuth()` now wraps `supabase.auth.signOut({ scope: 'local' })` in `withTimeout()` using existing `AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_TIMEOUT_MS` (3500ms) ‚Äî prevents hung AsyncStorage operations from leaving `isLoading` true forever. `finally` block always sets `isLoading` to false
  - **`expo/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`**: Added 5-second `loadingTimedOut` state ‚Äî if `isLoading` stays true for >5s, forces `effectiveLoading` to false so auth guard proceeds. Added "Loading IVX‚Ä¶" text below spinner. Uses `effectiveLoading = isLoading && !loadingTimedOut` pattern
- `app.config.ts` bumped to versionCode 18, buildMarker `IVX_BUNDLE_2026_07_15_BUILD_18_STARTUP_FIX`, buildTimestamp `2026-07-15T06:30:00.000000+00:00`
- TypeScript passes clean (0 errors) after all build 18 changes
- Build 18 APK built successfully:
  - `expo prebuild --clean --platform android` completed
  - `gradlew :app:assembleRelease` ‚Äî BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 1m 4s, 397 tasks
  - package `com.ivxholdings.app`, versionCode 18, versionName 1.4.3, 84MB (87,529,124 bytes)
  - Bundle verification: `preventAutoHideAsync` (2 matches), `hideAsync` (2 matches), "taking longer than expected" (1), "failed to start" (1), "Startup timeout reached" (1), "Cold-launch signOut" (1), "TabsLayout" (1), "initAuth" (1), "withTimeout" (1) ‚Äî all confirmed in `index.android.bundle`
  - SHA-256: `edb9cf86cfa9935e3524389f6cb019dea25932523c08168fe1ff175709fe2cb1`
  - Copied to `/home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build18.apk`
- **gofile.io link failed** ‚Äî `https://gofile.io/d/bjK29i` serves `text/html` (12KB page), NOT a direct APK download. Android browser loads the page but cannot download the file. This was the cause of user's download failure.
- **Build 18 APK re-uploaded to litterbox.catbox.moe**: `https://litter.catbox.moe/iin8jx.apk`
  - Verified: HTTP 200, `content-type: application/octet-stream`, `content-length: 87529124`, `accept-ranges: bytes`
  - Full 84MB download verified: SHA-256 matches source APK exactly
  - Link expires in 72 hours
- `DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json` updated with build 18 details, verified download URL, and full audit results
- `.rork/plans/build-one-unified-ivx-holdings-android-a_1784077962623.plan.md` updated for build 18
- Watchman disabled (`chmod -x /usr/local/bin/watchman`) ‚Äî Metro uses polling fallback
- File host testing results:
  - gofile.io: serves HTML page, not direct download ‚Äî REJECTED
  - catbox.moe: "Invalid uploader" ‚Äî REJECTED
  - pixeldrain: requires API auth ‚Äî REJECTED
  - transfer.sh, file.io, tmp.sh, envs.sh, filebin.net, uguu.se, 0x0.st, ufile.io, send.cm, wormhole.app: all failed (timeouts, redirects, 413, 503, or empty responses)
  - litterbox.catbox.moe: SUCCESS ‚Äî direct APK download confirmed
  - Supabase Storage upload attempted but failed: 413 Payload too large (87MB exceeds limits even after bucket creation, tus resumable upload also failed with "Invalid upload-metadata")

### In Progress
- **User reports app STILL not working** ‚Äî sent third screen recording (82 frames, 41 seconds) showing both Expo Go and APK failing. Need to identify actual runtime error, not just startup timing fixes.
- Was reading source files (`_layout.tsx`, `(tabs)/_layout.tsx`, `auth-context.tsx`, `login.tsx`, `access-control.ts`, `DiagnosticErrorBoundary.tsx`, `open-access.ts`) to find runtime crash cause when summary was requested
- Screen recording frames extracted but webFetch only returned URLs without descriptions ‚Äî could not determine exact error shown on screen

### Blocked
- EXPO_TOKEN not set ‚Äî EAS cloud build unavailable
- EXPO_PUBLIC_EAS_PROJECT_ID not set ‚Äî defaults to placeholder
- GitHub token expired (HTTP 401) ‚Äî cannot push to GitHub repo, Render backend stuck at `0b37191f`
- Real-device testing cannot be done in sandbox ‚Äî user must test installed APK on physical device
- Android logcat and screenshot of first screen require a physical device or emulator ‚Äî not available in sandbox
- Cannot view screen recording content ‚Äî webFetch returns image URLs but no visual descriptions, so exact on-screen error is unknown
- Supabase Storage cannot host the APK ‚Äî 87MB exceeds upload limits for both POST and tus resumable methods
- User did not provide SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY when requested (it was found in `android-ivx-holdings/.env` instead)

## Key Decisions
- **Build 18 root cause #1 (black screen)**: `expo-splash-screen` was installed (`~31.0.13`) but NEVER imported or called. Android dismisses native splash before JS bundle loads ‚Üí black screen. Fixed with `preventAutoHideAsync()` + `hideAsync()`.
- **Build 18 root cause #2 (infinite loading)**: `initAuth()` had no timeout. `supabase.auth.signOut({ scope: 'local' })` does async AsyncStorage ops ‚Äî if slow on Android, `isLoading` stays true forever. Constant `AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_TIMEOUT_MS = 3500` existed at line 39 but was never used. Fixed by wrapping signOut in `withTimeout()`.
- **Build 18 root cause #3 (no safety net)**: TabsLayout loading spinner had no timeout ‚Äî showed indefinitely if `isLoading` never resolved. Fixed with 5-second `loadingTimedOut` state that forces auth guard to proceed.
- **Build 17 root cause (router)**: `app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx` had NO auth guard ‚Äî app opened to home without checking `isAuthenticated`. Fixed with `useEffect` redirect to `/login`.
- **Download link failure**: gofile.io serves HTML landing pages, not direct file downloads. Android browsers cannot download APKs from gofile.io `/d/` URLs. Switched to litterbox.catbox.moe which serves `application/octet-stream` directly.
- Open access mode (`isOpenAccessModeEnabled()`) bypasses the auth guard ‚Äî only true in development builds
- `redirectAttemptedRef` prevents duplicate redirects and resets when user becomes authenticated
- Cold launch signs out persisted Supabase session ‚Äî owner MUST enter credentials every time
- `manualOwnerLoginRef` gates `onAuthStateChange` ‚Äî ignores all session events unless set true by manual login
- Building APK locally via `expo prebuild` + `gradlew assembleRelease` since EAS cloud is blocked
- **NEW ISSUE**: User reports app still not working even after build 18 fixes. The startup timing fixes may not address the actual runtime error. Need to investigate whether there's a JS crash, provider initialization failure, or other runtime error causing the app to fail on both Expo Go and standalone APK.

## Next Steps
1. **Identify the actual runtime error** ‚Äî user's third screen recording shows app still failing on both Expo Go and APK. The build 18 fixes addressed timing issues but there may be a deeper runtime crash (JS error, provider failure, navigation error)
2. Try to run the app in Metro/Expo Go mode locally to reproduce the crash and capture the error
3. If the DiagnosticErrorBoundary or startup error screen is showing, read the error text from the recording more carefully
4. Consider temporarily simplifying the root `_layout.tsx` to render a minimal screen (just a Text "IVX") to isolate whether the crash is in a provider or in the router
5. User must test build 18 APK from `https://litter.catbox.moe/iin8jx.apk` on real device ‚Äî the gofile.io link was broken but litterbox link is verified working

## Critical Context
- Git HEAD SHA: `e3149d5c995d8e3d95a205d9d4ce4ae65a0958f3` (`e3149d5`)
- Build 18 APK at `/home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build18.apk` (84MB, versionCode 18, SHA-256 `edb9cf86...`)
- **Working download link**: `https://litter.catbox.moe/iin8jx.apk` (verified HTTP 200, application/octet-stream, 87,529,124 bytes, SHA-256 matches, expires in 72h)
- **Broken download link**: `https://gofile.io/d/bjK29i` (serves HTML page, NOT direct APK)
- Build 17 APK at `/home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build17.apk` ‚Äî uploaded to `https://gofile.io/d/V1vw3Y` (also broken ‚Äî HTML page)
- Build 16 APK at `/home/user/rork-app/ivx-holdings-v1.4.3-build16.apk` and `https://litter.catbox.moe/ti5mjx.apk` (likely expired)
- `aapt2` at `/home/user/.gradle/caches/8.14.1/transforms/.../aapt2` for APK verification
- Supabase production URL: `https://kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb.supabase.co` (project ref `kvclcdjmjghndxsngfzb`)
- Supabase service role key found in `android-ivx-holdings/.env` line 7 (JWT starting with `eyJhbGci...`)
- Supabase storage buckets: `app-releases` (public, 50MB limit), `apk-releases` (public, created but also limited), `chat-uploads` (public, no limit), `deal-photos`, `landing-page`, `ivx-deliverables`
- Supabase client `lib/supabase.ts` uses Proxy pattern for live client delegation, `persistSession: true` with AsyncStorage adapter
- `AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_TIMEOUT_MS = 3500` defined at line 39 of `auth-context.tsx` ‚Äî now actually used in `initAuth()` via `withTimeout()`
- `withTimeout` utility at line 433 of `auth-context.tsx` ‚Äî resolves with `fallbackValue` after timeout
- `expo-splash-screen` version `~31.0.13` in package.json
- No font loading calls anywhere in the app (`useFonts`, `Font.loadAsync` not used)
- Metro bundler works with Watchman disabled ‚Äî uses polling fallback
- Gradle 8.14.3, JDK 17.0.13, Kotlin 2.1.20, Android SDK 35/36 build-tools available
- User has sent 3 screen recordings total:
  - Recording 1 & 2 (same video, 46 frames, 23s): showed gofile.io download page not downloading APK
  - Recording 3 (82 frames, 41s): shows app still not working on device ‚Äî exact error unknown

## Relevant Files
- `expo/app/_layout.tsx`: Root layout ‚Äî **BUILD 18**: added SplashScreen control, 5s startup timeout with visible error screen, try/catch on all startup tasks. Providers: I18n ‚Üí Auth ‚Üí Analytics ‚Üí IPX ‚Üí Email, each wrapped in `ProviderBoundary`
- `expo/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx`: Tabs layout ‚Äî **BUILD 17+18**: auth guard redirects to `/login` when unauthenticated, 5s loading safety timeout with `effectiveLoading`, "Loading IVX‚Ä¶" text
- `expo/lib/auth-context.tsx`: Core auth context ‚Äî **BUILD 16+18**: `initAuth()` signs out on cold launch wrapped in `withTimeout(AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_TIMEOUT_MS)`, `onAuthStateChange` gated by `manualOwnerLoginRef`, `isLoading` initial state `true`
- `expo/app.config.ts`: Expo config ‚Äî versionCode 18, buildMarker `IVX_BUNDLE_2026_07_15_BUILD_18_STARTUP_FIX`, sourceCommitSha `e3149d5...`
- `expo/lib/supabase.ts`: Supabase singleton ‚Äî Proxy-based live client, `persistSession: true`, AsyncStorage adapter, `autoRefreshToken: true`, `flowType: 'pkce'`
- `expo/lib/open-access.ts`: `isOpenAccessModeEnabled()` ‚Äî false in production
- `expo/shared/ivx/access-control.ts`: `getIVXAccessControlConfig()` ‚Äî `openAccessEnabled` false in production, contains hardcoded production Supabase URL and anon key as fallbacks
- `expo/app/login.tsx`: Login screen ‚Äî redirects to `/(tabs)/(home)/home` when `isAuthenticated` (line 553-574), open-access bypass (line 536-547)
- `expo/components/DiagnosticErrorBoundary.tsx`: Shows full crash error + stack trace on screen ‚Äî may be what user is seeing in recording 3
- `expo/app/(tabs)/(home)/_layout.tsx`: Home segment Stack layout ‚Äî no auth check (relies on parent tabs guard)
- `android-ivx-holdings/.env`: Contains Supabase service role key, owner email/password, Supabase URL ‚Äî used as source of credentials
- `DEPLOYMENT_PROOF.json`: Updated with build 18 details, verified litterbox download URL, full audit results
- `.rork/plans/build-one-unified-ivx-holdings-android-a_1784077962623.plan.md`: Project plan tracking all tasks ‚Äî updated for build 18
